cirrus.overview:
# Cirrus（雲中君） — Assassin

## Overview

- **Role:** Assassin (Aerial Diver / Displacer)
- **Lane:** Jungle
- **Difficulty:** ★★★★☆
- **Title:** 流雲之翼 (Wings of Flowing Cloud)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 云中君)

> Cirrus received a major rework. He no longer has the "Breath" limitation (no more grounded "walking chicken"). He now has **permanent flight** and a reworked ultimate with a hard **"grab-and-carry" CC mechanic**. At level 4+, he ignores all terrain permanently.

---

## Core Mechanics (Post-Rework)

### Permanent Flight & Terrain Ignore

From level 4 onward, Cirrus **permanently flies** and can pass through all walls. His jungle clear, rotation, and flank speed are among the fastest in the game.

### Tear Stacks (Passive)

Basic attacks stack **Tear (bleed)** on enemies. At 4 stacks, the target takes continuous damage and Cirrus **recovers HP**. Tear damage can **critically strike**.

### Ultimate: Sky Dive + Grab (S3)

Cirrus ascends into the sky (**untargetable**), then dives down to **grab one enemy Hero, carrying them into the air** (similar to Fuzi's rope + Donghuang's suppress). He can **move the grabbed enemy a short distance**. This is the ultimate tower-dive, backline-kidnap, and peel tool.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
cirrus.tips:
# Cirrus Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Permanent Flight (Level 4+)

From level 4, Cirrus **never lands**. He can fly through all terrain, making him the fastest jungler for rotations and flanking. Rush level 4 as quickly as possible.

### 2. Tear Stacks = Crit Bleed

Basic attacks stack **Tear** (max 4 stacks). At 4 stacks: continuous bleed damage + HP recovery for Cirrus. **Tear damage can crit** — paired with Infinity Edge, every bleed tick becomes a massive critical hit.

### 3. Ultimate: Grab & Carry (S3)

S3 makes Cirrus **untargetable** as he ascends, then dives to **grab one enemy Hero and carry them into the air**. You can **move the grabbed enemy a short distance**. This is the strongest displacement tool in the game — use it to:
- **Kidnap squishies into your team:** Grab → pull toward your allies → instant deletion
- **Tower dive execute:** Grab enemy under tower → carry them OUT of tower range → finish
- **Peel for carries:** Grab diving Assassins off your backline

### 4. S1: Speed Boost & Engage

Hold S1 for continuous Movement Speed + Slow Immunity. Release to swoop forward. Use to close distance instantly. Hitting enemies reduces their damage against you.

### 5. S2: Ranged Feather Volley

Fires feathers in a cone — ranged damage + slow. Use to stack Tear from range before committing.

### 6. Skill Priority

**Max S1 first, then S2. Take ultimate whenever available.**

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Tower Dive: **S1 → S2 → S3 → AA×4**

1. S1 charge in at max speed
2. S2 ranged slow + stack Tear
3. S3 ascend (untargetable) → grab enemy under tower → carry them OUT
4. Land → basic attack to 4 stacks → Tear bleed finishes them

### Bush Ambush: **AA → S2 → AA → S1 → S3 → AA**

1. Start with auto-attack from bush
2. S2 immediately — 4 Tear stacks applied
3. Enemy Flashes/dashes → S1 pursuit
4. S3 grab to finish or pull them back

### Reverse Kidnap (S3 direction control): **S3 grab → Left Joystick toward team**

After grabbing with S3, immediately pull your left joystick **toward your team/tower**. The grabbed enemy moves with you — drop them into certain death.

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Pre-level-4 play safe:** Cirrus is vulnerable before permanent flight. Farm efficiently, avoid early skirmishes.
- **Tear crit is your power spike:** Rush Infinity Edge after Shadow Axe. It transforms your bleed from poke to execution.
- **S3 untargetable frames:** Use to dodge key abilities (Daji S2, Angela ultimate, tower shots).
- **Matchup:** Destroys immobile squishies (Lu Ban No.7, Xiao Qiao). Struggles vs instant CC (Zhang Liang S3) that can lock him during S1 swoop.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
cirrus.build:
# Cirrus Build & Items

> **Build philosophy:** Post-rework Cirrus is a Physical Assassin. His Tear bleed damage fully benefits from **Physical Penetration** and **Critical Rate** (bleed CAN crit). High Attack Speed helps stack Tear faster. Build path: **Crit + Penetration**.

---

## Recommended Jungle Build

| 1 | **Greedy Bite (贪婪之噬)** | Standard Assassin jungle item: Attack, Penetration, CDR. |
| 2 | **Berserker's Boots (急速战靴)** | Early Attack Speed for smoother kiting and faster Tear stacking. *Late game: swap to Resistance Boots (抵抗之靴) vs heavy CC.* |
| 3 | **Shadow Axe (暗影战斧)** | Core squishy-killer: CDR, Health, flat Physical Penetration. |
| 4 | **Infinity Edge (无尽战刃)** | **Cirrus's power-spike item.** Tear bleed damage CAN crit. Infinity Edge transforms every bleed tick into massive critical numbers. |
| 5 | **Master's Power (宗师之力)** or **Shadow Blade (影刃)** | **Master's Power:** enhanced AA after abilities for burst. **Shadow Blade:** Attack Speed + crit synergy for sustained Tear stacking. |
| 6 | **Pure Sky (纯净苍穹)** or **Fate's Edge (名刀·司命)** | Survival slot. S3 is untargetable but you're vulnerable on landing. Pure Sky's active DR ensures you survive the landing. |

---

Core needs: **Initial Critical Rate (synergy with Infinity Edge), Physical Penetration, Movement Speed.**

**Total:** ~86 Physical Penetration, +25 Physical Attack, **+6.4% Critical Rate**, +4.8% Physical Lifesteal, +7% Movement Speed. The early crit chance creates "faith crits" in early jungle skirmishes.

### Setup 2: "Max Speed Kiting"

| Slot | Rune | Qty | Stats per Rune |

| 🔴 Red | Mutation (异变) | ×10 | Physical Attack +2, Physical Pen +3.6 |

Cirrus was reworked. If you're searching old guides, you may find outdated "Breath management" tips for the pre-rework version. The safest method:

consort-yu.overview:
# Consort Yu（虞姬） — Marksman

## Overview

- **Role:** Marksman (Anti-Physical / Duelist ADC)
- **Lane:** Farm Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 森之靈風 (Forest Spirit Wind)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 虞姬)

> Consort Yu is a uniquely designed, high-ceiling Marksman. Her signature mechanic is her S2: **2 seconds of complete Physical Damage immunity**. This makes her the natural counter to every Physical Assassin, burst Fighter, and even most enemy ADCs. She also has a built-in CC-cleanse on S2 activation and a S3 that stuns + creates distance + grants Attack Speed.

---

## Core Mechanics

### S2: Physical Immunity (The Entire Kit)

S2 grants: **+MS, 2s Physical Damage immunity, slow cleanse, and CC immunity on the activation frame.** If Daji's charm or Hou Yi's bird is about to hit, S2 right before impact negates the CC entirely. **Never waste S2 for movement speed** — it's your only survival tool.

### S3: Stomp + Create Distance

S3 dashes to target, **stuns 0.5s**, slows, then jumps backward. During the jump, **CC-immune**. Landing grants **+4 seconds of massive Attack Speed**. When Han Xin, Wukong, or Lanling Wang dives you: S3 stomp → auto-jump backward → S2 if needed → auto-attack them down from safety.

### S1: Long-Range Poke

S1 is a penetrating arrow with high base damage and extreme range. Use to farm safely vs oppressive laners (Arli, Goya), bush-check, or finish fleeing enemies.

### Late-Game Transformation

Early game: S1 poke mage. Late game with crit/AS items: your AAs become devastating. S2 Physical immunity + S3 self-peel means you win duels other ADCs can't.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

**Hard-countered by:** Magic burst (Sikong Zhen, Mi Yue, Luna, Kongming, Mai Shiranui). S2 does NOT block Magic Damage or True Damage. When facing magic-heavy comps, build Magic Cloak early.
*Waiting for community input…*
consort-yu.tips:
# Consort Yu Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S2: Never Waste It on Movement Speed

S2 is your life. It grants: **2s Physical immunity + CC cleanse on activation + slow removal + double-arrow on next AA.** Never use it to rotate faster. Without S2, enemy Assassins (Li Bai, Jing, Lam) WILL tower-dive you. Only cast S2 when:
- An Assassin engages you
- You see a CC projectile flying at you (Daji charm, Hou Yi bird)
- You need to immune a tower shot

### 2. S2 Cleanses CC on Activation Frame

The first ~0.1s of S2 grants **CC immunity**. If you time it right as a CC hits, you negate it entirely. Practice this timing — it's the hallmark of a great Consort Yu.

### 3. S3: Anti-Dive + Offensive Tool

When dived by melee Assassins: **S3 their face.** The stomp stuns them, then you auto-jump backward while CC-immune. You're now at safe range with +4s Attack Speed buff. Turn and kill them.

### 4. S1: Farm Safe vs Lane Bullies

Vs oppressive laners (Arli, Goya): max S1 and farm from long range. S1's damage and range let you last-hit safely without entering their threat zone.

### 5. Magic Damage = Your Kryptonite

S2 blocks **only Physical Damage**. Magic burst (Kongming, Mai Shiranui, Sikong Zhen, Luna) and True Damage (Lu Bu) ignore it entirely. When facing magic-heavy comps, build Magic Cloak early and position extra carefully.

---

## Signature Combos

### Anti-Dive Counter-Kill: **S3 → AA → S1 → AA**

1. Enemy Assassin dives you
2. **S3** stomp them → stun + auto-jump backward
3. **AA** with S3's Attack Speed buff
4. **S1** as they try to flee
5. **S2** saved for if another enemy joins or you need Physical immunity

### Full Burst Engage: **S2 → S3 → AA×n → S1**

1. S2 (Physical immunity + double-arrow ready)
2. S3 stomp (stun + jump back + AS buff)
3. AA repeatedly with S3's 4-second AS steroid
4. S1 to finish at range

### Bush Ambush: **S1 → S3 → AA**

1. S1 from bush (full damage, no warning)
2. S3 immediately after (stun + AS buff)
3. AA until dead

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S2 double-arrow passive triggers on-hit effects.** Use it to get an extra Doomsday/Ice Grip proc.
- **S3 can cross thin walls.** If positioned right, you can jump over jungle walls to escape or chase.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs Physical comps. Struggles vs Magic-heavy teams.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

da-qiao.overview:
# Da Qiao（大喬） — Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Support (Strategic / Global Teleport)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 滄海之曜 (Light of the Sea)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 大乔)

> Da Qiao is a strategically dominant, game-deciding utility Support. Her unique **S2 "Elevator"** (teleport allies to base for full heal) and **S3/S4 global teleport** make her a staple in high-rank and pro play. She doesn't require mechanics — she requires **macro awareness and placement genius**.

---

## Core Mechanics

### S2: Elevator (Teleport to Base)

Summons a circle. After 4 seconds, all allies inside are **teleported to base and fully healed**. **Never place S2 under fighting allies** — they'll be accidentally sent home. Place it **to the side, in bushes, or on the tank's retreat path.**

### S4: Global Rally (Teleport to Fight)

Summons a large circle. **All allies across the map can tap to teleport directly to it.** Also grants Attack Speed to allies inside. Use for: team-wide ganks, objective contests, split-push escapes, and tower sieges.

### S2 → S4 Combo (Full Heal + Instant Return)

1. Place **S2** in safe spot
2. 1-2 seconds before S2 activates, cast **S4** on the battlefield
3. Team takes S2 home (full heal) → immediately **tap S4 to teleport back**
4. Entire team returns to the fight at full HP in under 5 seconds

### S1: Knockback + S3: Silence

- **S1:** Tidal wave knocks enemies back — interrupts Mozi S3, Dun Shan shield, Zhong Kui S3.
- **S3:** Continuous **Silence** zone. Drop on Gan & Mo, Shangguan, or diving Assassins to prevent them from casting.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

God-tier with: Fuzi (split-push), Milady (tower siege), any team-comp that values global presence.
*Waiting for community input…*
da-qiao.tips:
# Da Qiao Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S2 Placement: NEVER Under Fighting Allies

This is the #1 Da Qiao mistake. S2 under fighting allies = they get sent home mid-fight ("actor elevator"). **Always place S2 to the side, in bushes, or on the tank's retreat path.** Let allies CHOOSE to step in.

### 2. S2 → S4 Combo: Full HP Team Returns

1. Place **S2** in a safe spot behind your team
2. ~2 seconds in, cast **S4** where the fight is happening
3. Team takes S2 home → full HP + Mana → **immediately tap S4**
4. Entire team is back in the fight at full resources within 5 seconds
5. This is Da Qiao's most game-breaking mechanic

### 3. S3 Silence: Shut Down Casters

S3 creates a **continuous Silence zone**. Drop it on:
- Gan & Mo (can't cast swords)
- Shangguan (can't dash)
- Diving Assassins (can't combo)
The Silence prevents ALL ability casts while they stand in it.

### 4. S1: Interrupt Channeled Abilities

S1's knockback wave interrupts:
- Mozi S3 (ultimate)
- Dun Shan S1 (shield)
- Zhong Kui S3 (ultimate)
- Zhang Liang S3 (ultimate)

### 5. S4: Not Just for Fighting

S4's global teleport has multiple strategic uses:
- **Split-push escape:** Push sidelane → S4 to join team when enemies respond
- **Objective contest:** Teleport whole team to Dragon/Baron instantly
- **Tower siege:** S4 at enemy tower + Attack Speed buff = fast demolition

---

## Signature Combo

### Full Team Reset: **S2 → S4**

1. Team is low after extended fight
2. Place S2 behind retreat path
3. Cast S4 on the objective/enemy position
4. Team heals to full → teleports back → wins fight with resource advantage

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S4 grants Attack Speed.** Use it when sieging towers — the AS buff speeds up demolition significantly.
- **Coordinate S2 timing.** In solo queue, quick-chat "Retreat" or "Gather" before S2 activates so teammates know to step in.
- **Da Qiao + Fuzi/Milady is unfair.** Global split-push + instant team rotations = unanswerable map pressure.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

da-yu.overview:
# Da Yu（大禹） — Tank

## Overview

- **Role:** Tank (Terrain Control / Frontline)
- **Lane:** Clash Lane / Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 鼎鎮山河 (Stabilizer of Rivers & Mountains)
- **Region:** CN server exclusive (大禹)

> Da Yu is a strategically powerful Clash Lane / Roaming Tank whose kit revolves around **flood control and terrain manipulation.** He can create temporary walls to block enemies, has a stacking passive that grants defenses and culminates in a devastating **%Max HP True Damage** enhanced AA, and his S3 channels an Unstoppable tidal wave with massive DR.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: Unyielding Water Force

Taking damage or casting abilities builds Water Force. Higher Force = **higher Physical & Magic Defense.** At max Force, the next AA consumes all stacks for a massive AoE slam dealing **%Max HP True Damage.** Pre-stack Force before engaging.

### Terrain Wall: Strategic Blockade

Da Yu can create a temporary "earth dam" or "water wall" (similar to Nuwa's matrix or Yi Xing's cage). Use to: block escape paths of immobile carries, isolate enemy Assassins from your backline, or create chokepoints in jungle corridors.

### S3: Unstoppable + High DR During Channel

S3 channels a massive tidal wave. During the channel: **Unstoppable + high DR.** Use reactively — when Daji, Lady Zhen, or Mozi fires CC, cast S3 to ignore it and counter-engage.

### Pre-Stack Force Before Ganking

Before roaming or bush-camping: use S1/S2 on jungle camps or into empty air to build Force close to max. When the fight starts, your first AA is the True Damage slam.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
da-yu.tips:
# Da Yu Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Pre-Stack Water Force Before Fighting

Force grants defenses and culminates in a **%Max HP True Damage AA.** Before engaging: S1/S2 jungle camps or empty air to build Force to near-max. Enter the fight with the True Damage slam ready.

### 2. Terrain Wall: Block, Don't Just Place

The wall is temporary but game-changing. Uses:
- Block enemy retreat paths (they must Flash or die)
- Isolate enemy Assassins from your backline
- Create chokepoints in jungle corridors
- Protect your ADC by walling between them and divers

### 3. S3: Reactive, Not Proactive

S3 grants Unstoppable + high DR during channel. Don't open with it — save for: Daji charm, Lady Zhen freeze, Mozi S2 stun, or any telegraphed CC. **Cast S3 as the CC flies at you** — you'll ignore it and counter-engage.

### 4. Max Force AA = Your Biggest Damage

The Force-consuming AA deals **%Max HP True Damage.** Don't waste it on minions. Always target enemy Heroes (preferably tanks — %HP shreds them).

### 5. S1 + S2 for Continuous CC

S1 is primary damage and CC. S2 creates terrain or slows. Chain: S2 (block escape) → S1 (knockup/stun into the wall) → Force AA → S3 (if needed).

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Engage: **S2 → S1 → AA (Force) → S3**

1. S2 terrain wall (blocks escape path)
2. S1 knockup/stun (enemy hits wall = extended CC)
3. AA with max Force (True Damage slam)
4. S3 tidal wave (cleanup or counter-CC)

### Backline Protection: **S1 → S2 → S3 → AA**

1. Assassin dives your ADC
2. S1 knock them up at your ADC's feet
3. S2 wall between Assassin and ADC
4. S3 if more enemies follow
5. AA Force slam to finish the isolated Assassin

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S2 wall can be placed diagonally.** This maximizes the blocking surface area.
- **Force stacks decay slowly out of combat.** Keep hitting things to maintain stacks.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs immobile comps. Struggles vs hyper-mobile Heroes who ignore terrain (perma-flight Cirrus, long-dash Heroes).

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

daji.overview:
# Daji（妲己） — Mage

## Overview

- **Role:** Mage (Single-Target Burst / Bush Ambusher)
- **Lane:** Mid Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★☆☆☆☆
- **Title:** 魅力之狐 (Charming Fox)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 妲己)

> Daji is the game's most classic pure single-target burst Mage. Her kit is simple and brutal: S2 is an **undodgeable lock-on stun**, S3 fires 5 homing foxfires, and her passive **shreds enemy Magic Defense** per ability hit. Her entire playstyle revolves around bush camping and one-combo deletion. Mid-late game, a fed Daji turns any unwarded bush into a death sentence.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: Magic Defense Shred

Each ability hit reduces the target's Magic Defense (max 3 stacks). The more hits in a combo, the closer to True Damage. **This is why combo ORDER matters.**

### S2: Undodgeable Lock-On Stun

S2 is a point-and-click stun — **impossible to dodge without invulnerability frames.** Use to: stop divers (Ma Chao, Guan Yu, Lam), initiate kills, or interrupt channeled abilities.

### S3: Foxfires Split Between Targets

S3 fires 5 foxfires. **If multiple enemies are nearby, they split — drastically reducing single-target damage.** Always ensure your target is ISOLATED (no minions, no nearby allies) before S3.

### Bush Camping = Your Identity

Daji is weak in open teamfights. Her power lies in **bush ambushes.** Clear wave → rotate to enemy jungle or river bushes → wait. One passing squishy = one kill.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
daji.tips:
# Daji Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Combo Order Matters (Passive Shred)

Passive shreds Magic Defense per hit. Correct order: **S2 → S3 → S1.** S2 stuns. S3's 5 foxfires stack the debuff to max. S1 finishes with near-True Damage. Wrong order (S2 → S1 → S3) does significantly less damage.

### 2. Drag S2 — Don't Auto-Cast

Auto-casting S2 targets the nearest enemy (usually the tank). In teamfights, **drag S2's cast wheel** onto the enemy ADC or Mage. This is the #1 skill gap for Daji players.

### 3. Isolate Before S3

S3's foxfires split between all nearby targets. Before casting S3: ensure your target is **alone** — no minions, no monsters, no other enemy Heroes nearby. A split S3 can't kill.

### 4. Bush Camp: Your True Power

Daji's lane presence is weak. Her strength is **bush ambushes.** Clear mid wave → rotate to enemy Red/Blue buff bush or river bush → wait. Enemy squishy passes = S2 → S3 → S1 → dead. Repeat.

### 5. S1 + Flash: Surprise Finisher

S1 has a brief cast delay. Press S1 → **Flash forward** during the animation. The wave extends to your Flash destination. Surprise kills on fleeing enemies.

---

## Signature Combos

### Standard Delete: **S2 → S3 → S1**

1. S2 lock-on stun (guaranteed)
2. S3 5 foxfires (stacks passive to max)
3. S1 (near-True Damage finisher)
Optimal for 95% of situations.

### Bush Speed Kill: **S3 → S2 → S1**

1. S3 first (longer range — fires before they react)
2. S2 mid-S3 (stun lands while foxfires travel)
3. S1 finish
Enemies with escape skills (Arli, Li Bai) can't react.

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S2 interrupts dashes.** Time it vs Lam, Guan Yu, Ma Chao mid-charge.
- **Post-combo, you're vulnerable for ~5 seconds.** Hide in a bush until cooldowns return.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs solo squishies. Struggles vs tanky teams that body-block and survive the combo.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

dasiming.overview:
# Dasiming（大司命） — Fighter / Tank

## Overview

- **Role:** Fighter / Tank (Soul Chain Jungler)
- **Lane:** Jungle / Clash Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 肅歸之戈 (Spear of Solemn Return)
- **Region:** CN server exclusive (大司命)

> Dasiming is a mythic-level jungle dominator. His AAs are a **soul chain tether** — continuous damage while he moves freely (no attack-move needed). His S1 creates a path that lets him **ignore terrain and pass through walls.** His S3 slams down and **summons a clone of any enemy Hero hit** to fight for him. He's a sustain-drain brawler, not a burst Assassin.

---

## Core Mechanics

### AA: Soul Chain Tether (Move While Attacking)

Dasiming's AA is a tether that continuously damages enemies for up to 3 seconds. **You don't need to keep pressing AA** — just hold the joystick to move and the chain persists. The final tick deals massive burst damage. The tether is difficult to break with dashes/Flash — only extreme range breaks it.

### S1: Terrain-Ignoring Path

S1 creates a path. Dasiming gains massive MS on it. **If S1 crosses a wall, he can walk through it.** This makes him one of the fastest junglers for rotations and escapes.

### S2: Mark → Multi-Tether

S2 strikes the ground, leaving marks. Enemies stepping on marks cause Dasiming's next AA to **tether multiple enemies simultaneously.** Farm multiple jungle camps or drain multiple enemies in teamfights.

### S3: Clone Summon

S3 slams down (AoE damage + heal). **Any enemy Hero hit spawns a clone** that fights for Dasiming — the clone has a portion of the original's Attack. S3 an enemy ADC and their clone shreds their own team.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dasiming.tips:
# Dasiming Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. AA Tether: Move, Don't Mash

Dasiming's AA is a continuous tether. **Don't keep pressing the AA button** — press once, then focus entirely on movement. The tether persists for up to 3 seconds while you freely reposition. The final tick is the burst.

### 2. S2 → AA: Multi-Tether (Drain Everyone)

S2 leaves marks. When enemies step on them, your next AA **tethers ALL marked enemies simultaneously.** In teamfights: S2 into the enemy cluster → AA → you're draining 3+ enemies at once.

### 3. S1: Wall-Hack Jungle Rotations

S1 creates a path. If it crosses terrain: **you walk through the wall.** Use for: fastest jungle clears, creative gank angles, escapes over walls. Master the path placement — this is Dasiming's signature mobility.

### 4. S3: Clone the Enemy ADC

S3 summons a clone of any enemy Hero hit. **Always S3 the enemy ADC or fed carry.** Their clone attacks with a portion of their stats — an S3'd Hou Yi clone can solo-kill Hou Yi's own Support.

### 5. Tether Ignores Dashes and Flash

The soul chain tether is hard to break. Enemies who Flash or dash stay tethered unless they go beyond max range. Use this to tower-dive: tether outside tower → walk in as the tether ticks → final tick kills → walk out.

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Gank: **S1 → S2 → AA → S3 → AA**

1. S1 wall-hack path into lane
2. S2 mark on the enemy
3. AA tether (continuous damage while you walk with them)
4. S3 slam (clone + heal)
5. AA finish

### Teamfight: **S2 → AA → S3 → S1**

1. S2 into enemy cluster (marks multiple)
2. AA multi-tether (draining everyone)
3. S3 (clone their ADC + massive heal)
4. S1 wall-hack to chase or escape

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Tether works on towers?** No. But you can tether enemies from outside tower range and walk in for the final tick.
- **S3 clone inherits on-hit effects.** Items like Doomsday and Blood Weeper apply through the clone.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs immobile comps. Struggles vs burst that kills before tether ramps.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

dharma.overview:
# Dharma（達摩） — Fighter

## Overview

- **Role:** Fighter (Wall Kick / Burst Engage)
- **Lane:** Clash Lane / Jungle
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 拳僧 (Fist Monk)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 达摩)

> Dharma is a high-burst, creative Fighter/Jungler. His S3 kicks enemies into walls — if they collide with ANY obstacle (terrain, turret rubble, Nuwa's matrix, Dun Shan's shield, Yi Xing's cage, even the crystal), S3 unlocks a second cast with **%missing HP execute damage.** Post-rework, his S1 now stores multiple charges, dramatically improving his engage range and consistency.

---

## Core Mechanics

### S3: Wall Kick (Any Obstacle Counts)

S3 kicks an enemy backward. If they hit **any obstacle**, S3 unlocks a second cast with **%missing HP execute.** Obstacles include: all terrain, turret rubble, Nuwa matrix, Dun Shan shield, Yi Xing's S3 cage, and the crystal.

### S1: Multi-Charge (Post-Rework)

S1 now stores charges. "S1 → S1" gives Dharma two long-range dashes without needing Flash. This dramatically improves his engage reliability.

### Passive: AA Heal + Defense

After S1/S2, the next 2 AAs become **fast double-punches** with bonus AS + HP restore. Always weave: S1 → AA×2 → S2 → AA×2. The healing is substantial — never dump all skills without AAs between them.

### S1 Angle → S3

Enemies rarely stand against walls conveniently. Technique: **S1 knockup → walk to their side/front during the airborne → S3** kicks them into the nearest wall. The S1 float gives you time to reposition.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dharma.tips:
# Dharma Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S3: Any Obstacle = Wall

S3's wall-kick works on: terrain, turret rubble, Nuwa's matrix, Dun Shan's shield, Yi Xing's S3 cage, and the crystal. **If it blocks movement, it's a wall.** Look for these in every fight.

### 2. S1 Knockup → Reposition → S3 Wall Kick

Enemies rarely stand conveniently against walls. Technique: S1 knockup → during their airborne frames, **walk to the side or behind them** → S3 to kick them into the nearest wall. The S1 float time gives you the window to reposition.

### 3. Always Weave S1/S2 → AA×2

S1 and S2 each grant 2 enhanced fast-punch AAs with bonus AS + HP restore. Never S1 → S2 → S3 in one burst. Always: **S1 → AA×2 → S2 → AA×2 → S3.** The healing from those AAs wins trades that pure abilities lose.

### 4. S1 Multi-Charge (Post-Rework)

S1 stores charges. "S1 → S1" gives two long-range dashes. This means Dharma no longer depends on Flash for engage. Use double-S1 to gap-close from off-screen.

### 5. S3 Second Cast = Execute

S3's second cast deals **%missing HP damage** — the lower they are, the harder it hits. Always full combo BEFORE S3 second cast to maximize the execute damage.

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Engage: **S1 → S1 → S3 → AA → S2 → AA×2 → S3 (execute)**

1. S1 double-dash into enemy backline
2. S3 kick into wall
3. AA (enhanced)
4. S2 armor shred + AA×2 (heal)
5. S3 second cast (execute)

### Bush Ambush: **S2 → S3 → AA×2 → S1 → S3 (execute)**

1. S2 from bush (armor shred)
2. S3 kick into wall
3. AA×2 (enhanced + heal)
4. S1 chase if they Flash
5. S3 second cast (execute)

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S1 float time is your S3 setup window.** Don't rush S3 after S1 — use the airborne frames to reposition.
- **S2 shreds armor.** Always S2 before the full S3 combo for maximum damage.
- **Matchup:** God-tier near walls and in jungle corridors. Struggles in open-field fights with no obstacles.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

di-renjie.overview:
# Di Renjie（狄仁傑） — Marksman

## Overview

- **Role:** Marksman (Kiting King / Self-Cleanse ADC)
- **Lane:** Farm Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★☆☆☆
- **Title:** 斷案大師 (Master Detective)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 狄仁杰)

> Di Renjie is called the "true Fighter" among Marksmen. He has the best self-cleanse in the game (S2 removes ALL debuffs), mixed Physical/Magic damage, and a stacking passive that grants up to **30% AS + 25% MS**. His S3 shreds **50% of the target's dual defenses.** Two viable builds: On-Hit Semi-Tank (highest winrate) and Crit Burst.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: Always Keep 5 Stacks

Each AA grants +6% AS +5% MS (max 5 stacks = **30% AS + 25% MS**). Always pre-stack on minions/air before fighting. The MS is his kiting engine.

### S2: Self-Cleanse (Never Waste)

S2 removes ALL debuffs: stuns, slows, Li Yuanfang's S1 mark, Dr Bian's poison, Luna's mark — everything. **Never use S2 for damage or wave clear.** Save it for enemy CC or when an Assassin dives you.

### S3: 50% Dual Defense Shred

S3 stuns AND **reduces target's Physical + Magic Defense by 50%.** Even a full-tank Zhang Fei becomes paper. Always S3 the enemy frontline before your team focuses them.

### Blue/Red Card: Bush Check

Every 3rd AA throws a blue (penetrate + slow) or red (AoE explode) card. AA bushes from range — if you see the slow effect or hear the hit sound, enemies are inside.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
di-renjie.tips:
# Di Renjie Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Pre-Stack 5 Passive Before Every Fight

Passive grants 30% AS + 25% MS at 5 stacks. Before any fight: AA minions, monsters, or even air to reach 5 stacks. The MS is what lets you kite circles around enemies. Never start a fight at 0 stacks.

### 2. S2: Your Lifeline — Never Waste It

S2 cleanses ALL debuffs. Save it for: Li Yuanfang's S1 mark (cleanses before detonation), Daji charm, Wang Zhaojun freeze, Luna's mark, Dr Bian's poison, tower shots. **Never S2 for wave clear or poke damage.**

### 3. S3: Shred Their Tank — Then Everyone Focuses

S3 reduces target's dual defenses by **50%.** In teamfights: S3 the enemy frontline (tank/bruiser). Your entire team now deals near-true damage to them. The tank melts in seconds.

### 4. Blue/Red Card Bush Check

Every 3rd AA throws a special card. AA into suspicious bushes. If you see blue card's slow trail or hear the hit sound → enemies inside. Free, safe bush-check without abilities.

### 5. S3 → AA → S2 → AA → S1 → AA

The perfect duel combo: S3 (stun + shred) → AA → if enemy CC's you → S2 (cleanse) → AA → S1 (finish). Unbeatable in 1v1.

---

## Signature Combo

### Counter-Kill vs Assassin: **S3 → AA → S2 → AA → S1**

1. Assassin dives you
2. S3 their face (stun + 50% defense shred)
3. AA while kiting backward
4. They use CC → S2 cleanse immediately
5. Continue AA → S1 to finish

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S2 has a tiny invulnerability window.** With perfect timing, you can dodge tower shots and even Zhuge Liang S3.
- **On-Hit build is meta.** It makes Di Renjie tanky enough to survive Assassin dives while still dealing high DPS.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs debuff-reliant Heroes (Li Yuanfang, Dr Bian). Struggles vs instant burst from stealth.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

dian-wei.overview:
# Dian Wei（典韋） — Fighter

## Overview

- **Role:** Fighter (Late-Game Melee Shredder)
- **Lane:** Jungle / Clash Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★☆☆☆
- **Title:** 狂戰士 (Berserker)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 典韦)

> Dian Wei is the quintessential late-game melee meat-grinder. His passive stacks permanent Physical Attack per kill/assist/CC-cleanse (max 6 stacks). At 6 stacks, his AA range increases and his damage becomes absurd — even with full tank items, he hits like a truck. His S3 makes ALL damage dealt (AAs, counter-damage from items, burn auras) deal 3% Max HP True Damage for 5 seconds.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: Rage Stacks (Permanent Scaling)

Gain 1 stack per CC-cleanse/kill/assist (max 6). Each stack grants **Physical Attack + AA range.** At 6 stacks, Dian Wei hits as hard as a full-damage Assassin even with tank items.

### S1: CC Cleanse (Don't Waste It)

S1 cleanses CC AND grants 2 Rage stacks. **NEVER use S1 for movement speed.** Save it for when the enemy CC's you → S1 cleanse → they've wasted their CC and you're now stronger.

### S2: Must Hit Target

S2 grants **+200% Attack Speed + 25-50% Physical Lifesteal** — but ONLY if it hits an enemy. If S2 misses: no AS, no lifesteal, dead Dian Wei. Use S3 slow or Smite first to guarantee S2 lands.

### S3: True Damage Engine

S3 deals initial damage + slow, then for **5 seconds, ALL damage Dian Wei deals (AAs, Thornmail reflect, Blazing Cape burn) deals additional 3% Max HP True Damage.** This is why tank Dian Wei still shreds everyone.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dian-wei.tips:
# Dian Wei Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S1: Save for CC Cleanse, NEVER for Speed

S1 cleanses CC AND gives 2 Rage stacks. If you waste S1 for movement speed, you'll get CC'd and die. Always: **get CC'd → S1 cleanse → surprise, you're stronger now and they have no CC left.**

### 2. S2: Guarantee the Hit

S2's +200% AS + 25-50% Lifesteal ONLY triggers on hit. If S2 misses: you're dead. Use S3 slow or Smite first, then S2 directly on top of the enemy. Never S2 from range.

### 3. S3 + Thornmail = Absurd True Damage

S3 makes ALL damage deal 3% Max HP True Damage for 5 seconds. This includes:
- Your AAs
- **Thornmail's reflect damage** (enemies kill themselves hitting you)
- **Blazing Cape's burn aura**
- Even Smite damage

Thornmail + S3 is Dian Wei's signature combo. The enemy ADC kills themselves by auto-attacking you.

### 4. Stack Passive Early, Scale Forever

Every CC-cleanse/kill/assist = +1 permanent Rage stack. Prioritize early skirmishes to stack up. A 6-stack Dian Wei at 20 minutes is a raid boss.

### 5. Don't Build Attack Speed (S2 Gives 200%)

S2 already gives +200% AS. Building more AS is completely wasted. Focus on: **Attack Damage, Critical Rate, tankiness.**

---

## Signature Combos

### Standard Engage: **S3 → S2 → AA spam → S1 (save for CC)**

1. S3 jump + slow
2. S2 on the slowed target (guaranteed hit)
3. AA with +200% AS + 25-50% Lifesteal + 3% Max HP True Damage
4. S1 saved — only use if CC'd

### Counter-CC Reverse-Kill: **Get CC'd → S1 (cleanse) → S3 → S2 → AA**

1. Enemy CC's you
2. S1 cleanse → +2 Rage stacks
3. S3 jump on them
4. S2 → AA them to death

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Thornmail is Dian Wei's real damage item.** S3 + Thornmail reflect = the enemy ADC dies in 3-4 auto-attacks against you.
- **10× Stealth (not Hunt) is mandatory.** Dian Wei doesn't need AS from runes. Stealth gives more AD + MS.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs Physical auto-attackers. Struggles vs kite-heavy Magic teams.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
dian-wei.build:
# Dian Wei Build & Items

> **Build philosophy:** S2 gives +200% AS — zero Attack Speed items needed. **S3 makes ALL damage deal 3% Max HP True Damage** — on-hit and reflect effects become deadly. Semi-tank is meta. CN server consensus: tank items with S3 synergy.

---

## Recommended Jungle Build

| Order | Item | Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| 1 | **Giant's Grip (巨人之握)** | HP stacking + burn aura. Burn triggers S3's 3% Max HP True Damage. |
| 2 | **Resistance Boots (抵抗之靴)** | Tenacity. Dian Wei needs to survive CC to S1-cleanse. |
| 3 | **Shadow Axe (暗影战斧)** | CDR + flat Penetration + Health. Only damage item needed. |
| 4 | **Thornmail (反伤刺甲)** | **Dian Wei's soul item!** Reflect damage triggers S3's 3% Max HP True Damage per tick. Enemy ADC kills themselves auto-attacking you. |
| 5 | **Master's Power (宗师之力)** or **Berserker's Armor (暴烈之甲)** | **Master's Power:** S1→AA burst for deleting squishies. **Berserker's:** stacking MS + damage. |
| 6 | **Eternal Night (永夜守护)** or **Magic Cloak (魔女斗篷)** | Magic defense. Prevents getting kited and poked out by Mages. |

---

## Arcana / Runes (铭文)

Zero Attack Speed needed (S2 gives +200%). **Pure Critical Rate + Penetration + Movement Speed.**

### Setup 1: "Calamity Stealth Burst" (CN Standard)

## Quick In-Game Setup

diaochan.overview:
# Diaochan（貂蟬） — Assassin / Mage

## Overview

- **Role:** Assassin / Mage (Sustained Skirmisher)
- **Lane:** Mid Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★★★☆
- **Title:** 絕世舞姬 (Peerless Dancer)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 貂蝉)

> Diaochan is a sustained-damage Magic Skirmisher who thrives in prolonged fights. She doesn't one-shot — she **dances inside her S3 circle**, stacking her passive marks to trigger chain explosions of **True Damage + 90% slow + self-healing.** With max CDR and Lifesteal, a well-played Diaochan can 1v5.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: 4-Stack Explosion

Abilities stack marks. At **4 stacks: AoE explosion** dealing True Damage + 90% slow + self-heal. If multiple enemies are marked together, chain explosions delete entire teams.

### S2: Invulnerability Frame + CD Reset

S2 dashes and fires 3 petals. **The dash animation has a brief invulnerability window** — dodge Zhuge Liang S3, tower shots, Musashi S3. **If ANY petal hits an enemy, S2 cooldown is reduced by 4 seconds.** If all petals miss, S2 has a long cooldown and you're dead.

### S3: Circle of Infinite Dancing

S3 creates a circle. **While Diaochan is inside, S1 and S2 cooldowns are massively reduced.** The circle also applies marks on creation and expiration.

### Diagonal Dancing

Never dash straight forward or backward. **Dash diagonally or in a triangle** around enemies — stay in the circle, dodge skillshots, and keep petals hitting.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
diaochan.tips:
# Diaochan Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S2 Invulnerability Frame = Your Survival

S2's dash has a brief **invulnerability window** (the disappear-reappear frames). Use to dodge:
- Zhuge Liang S3 (ultimate lock-on)
- Tower shots
- Musashi S3, Wukong bonk, Daji charm
Mastering S2 timing is what separates Diaochan gods from feeders.

### 2. S2 Petals Must Hit — NEVER Miss

S2's 3 petals reduce S2 cooldown by **4 seconds per hit.** If all 3 miss (dashing into a wall, no enemies nearby), S2 goes on full cooldown — and you die. Always dash TOWARD enemies, through them, or at angles where at least 1 petal connects.

### 3. Diagonal Dancing (Never Straight)

Never dash forward/backward in a straight line. Always dash **diagonally or in a triangle** around enemies. This keeps you in your S3 circle, dodges enemy skillshots (Gan & Mo swords), and maintains petal contact.

### 4. S3 Circle: Your Dance Floor

S3 massively reduces S1/S2 cooldowns while you're inside. **Never leave your circle during a fight.** If enemies try to kite you out, let them go — you're useless without the circle. Wait for S3 cooldown, then fight again on YOUR terms.

### 5. Grouped Enemies = Free Penta

If enemies cluster together, S1 and S2 mark multiple targets simultaneously. Chain explosions trigger every 4 stacks — with 3+ enemies, the chain reactions delete everyone instantly.

---

## Signature Combo

### Standard Dance: **S2 → S1 → S3 → S2 → S1 → S2 → S1...**

1. S2 dash in (stack marks + CD reset)
2. S1 (more marks)
3. S3 circle (marks on creation + super CDR)
4. Inside circle: alternate S2 → S1 → S2 → S1
5. Diagonal dashes only. Never leave the circle.

### Chase: **S2 → S1 → S3 (at max range toward enemy)**

1. S2 + S1 to stack marks and damage
2. Place S3 at max range IN the enemy's escape direction
3. The circle edge reduces your cooldowns immediately
4. S2 again to chase into the circle

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S3 applies marks on creation AND expiration.** Time your burst around these free stacks.
- **CDR is everything.** 40% CDR + S3 circle = near-infinite dashes.
- **Matchup:** God-tier vs melee-heavy comps that cluster. Struggles vs long-range poke that outranges her circle.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
diaochan.build:
# Diaochan Build & Items

> **Build philosophy:** Must reach **40% CDR** (S2 dance engine). Must have **Lifesteal** (melee skirmishing). Must build **semi-tank** — full AP gets deleted on engage. CN server consensus: **Semi-Tank CDR Lifesteal.**

---

## Recommended Semi-Tank Build

| Order | Item | Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| 1 | **Calm Boots (冷静之靴)** | CDR rush. Dance steps must flow. *Late: swap to Resistance Boots vs heavy CC.* |
| 2 | **Devourer's Tome (噬神之书)** | **Diaochan's soul item!** Massive Magic Lifesteal. Combined with runes = ~40% spell vamp. 1v5 sustain. |
| 3 | **Berserker's Armor (暴烈之甲)** or **Ominous Premonition (不祥征兆)** | Physical defense. **Berserker's:** stacking MS + damage while taking hits — perfect for prolonged dancing. |
| 4 | **Prophet's Hour (时之预言)** | AP + Max HP. Converts AP to dual defenses. Incredible cost-efficiency for melee Mage. |
| 5 | **Mask of Agony (痛苦面具)** or **Sunset Flow (日暮之流)** | **Mask:** percentage HP burn on multi-hit. **Sunset Flow:** penetration stacking vs magic resist. |
| 6 | **Magic Cloak (魔女斗篷)** or **Splendor (辉月)** | **Magic Cloak:** vs burst Mages. **Splendor:** Stasis for dodging fatal damage when S2 is on cooldown. |

---

## Arcana / Runes (铭文)

dolia.overview:
# Dolia（朵莉亞） — Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Support (Cooldown Refresher / Water Mage)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 人魚之歌 (Mermaid's Song)
- **Region:** CN server exclusive (朵莉亚)

> Dolia is a strategically invaluable utility Support with a unique **Mermaid Form** (water/land dual environment). Her game-breaking mechanic: **S3 refreshes the longest-cooldown ability of a targeted ally** — effectively giving them a second ultimate. She's the ultimate "combo enabler."

---

## Core Mechanics

### Mermaid Form (Water Environment)

When Dolia enters the river OR her own S2 water zone, she transforms into Mermaid Form: **+MS, %HP + %Mana regen per second, and AAs become AoE water blades.** Stay in water whenever possible.

### S1: Bush Check + Knockback

S1 fires 4 sound waves. **In water: the 4th wave becomes a massive knockback + stun.** The detection range is enormous — fire S1 toward suspicious bushes. If the wave changes behavior: enemies inside. Safest bush-check in the game.

### S3: Refresh Ally's Longest Cooldown

S3 refreshes the **longest-cooldown ability** of a targeted ally (usually their ultimate). Bind to a specific ally via the S3 UI wheel. **If unbonded, auto-targets nearest ally with longest CD.**

### Best S3 Partners

S3 enables "double ultimate" game-breaking combos with: **Lu Bu** (double sky slam), **Guitarist** (double nuclear concert), **Wu Zetian** (double global stun), **Shangguan** (double aerial assassination), **Wukong** (double staff), **Bai Qi** (double AoE taunt). Avoid pairing with heroes whose ults aren't game-changing (Marco, Arli, Han Xin).

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

God-tier with: Lu Bu, Guitarist, Wu Zetian, Shangguan, Bai Qi, Heino.
*Waiting for community input…*
dolia.tips:
# Dolia Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S3: Bind the Right Ally

S3 refreshes the longest-cooldown ability of the targeted ally. **Always bind your strongest ultimate combo partner.** Bind in fountain at match start via S3 wheel. Priority: Lu Bu > Guitarist > Wu Zetian > Shangguan > Bai Qi. Don't bind Marco, Arli, or Han Xin — their ults aren't game-changing.

### 2. S1 in Water = Knockback + Stun

S1's 4th wave becomes a knockback + stun when cast in water. Always: **S2 (create water) → S1 immediately.** The control is massive. Use to peel Assassins off your carry.

### 3. Stay in Water for Infinite Sustain

In Mermaid Form: %HP + %Mana regen per second. Between fights: stand in river or S2 water zone. Never recall for Mana or HP — Dolia sustains forever.

### 4. S1: Best Bush-Check in the Game

S1's detection range is enormous. Fire S1 toward any suspicious bush. If the wave changes (4th wave triggers knockback) → enemies inside. Zero risk, infinite range.

### 5. 40% CDR Is Mandatory

S3 shares cooldown with the ally's ability. If your CDR is low, S3 takes forever. Stack: Shadow Aegis aura (15%) + Calm Boots (15%) + runes (6-10%) = 40% cap.

---

## Signature Combo

### Anti-Dive Peel: **S2 → S1 → S3**

1. S2 create water at your feet (or you're in river)
2. S1 → 4th wave knockback + stun the diving Assassin
3. S3 your ADC (refreshes their escape/ultimate)

### Lane Sustain: **S2 → stand in water**

1. S2 at your feet
2. Stand there. HP + Mana regen.
3. Never recall.

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S1's 4th wave is directional.** Face the enemy when casting for maximum knockback distance.
- **Heino + Dolia = Time Freeze at level 15.** Dolia's S3 refreshes Heino's S3.
- **Matchup:** God-tier with high-impact ultimate allies. Struggles when team has no strong ultimate to refresh.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

donghuang.overview:
# Donghuang Taiyi（東皇太一） — Tank / Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Tank / Support (Suppression / HP Trader)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 噬滅日蝕 (Devouring Eclipse)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 东皇太一)

> Donghuang Taiyi is a strategically intimidating Roaming Tank. His core mechanic is his S3 **Suppression** — an uncleansable, Unstoppable-ignoring lockdown that **links his HP with the target** (any damage one takes is mirrored to the other). He is the ultimate counter to hyper-mobile, multi-dash, high-burst Heroes (Luna, Jing, Diaochan, Li Bai).

---

## Core Mechanics

### Energy Orbs + HP Recovery

S1 summons up to 3 rotating orbs. Orbs touching enemies deal Magic Damage + **restore Donghuang's HP**. The more enemies nearby, the more healing. In early jungle skirmishes, **walk clockwise around enemies** (orbs rotate counter-clockwise) to double the contact frequency and healing.

### S3: Contract Suppression (Ultimate Lockdown)

S3 is one of the strongest CC abilities in the game — **2.2s Suppression**. During the link, **any damage taken by either party is mirrored to the other**. Cannot be cleansed, cannot be blocked by Unstoppable. Donghuang MUST have more HP than the target to survive the trade.

### Tower Kill Exploit

If Donghuang suppresses an enemy under their own tower, **every tower shot Donghuang takes is mirrored to the enemy**. This lets Donghuang "borrow" the enemy tower to kill them. S2 → Flash → S3 under tower = guaranteed kill on squishy defenders.

### Pre-S3 S2 Combo

S2 throws all orbs for slow/stun. Casting S3 **instantly refills all 3 orbs**. Optimal sequence: **S2 (throw orbs for CC) → Flash → S3 (suppress)** — you get free S2 damage + 3 fresh orbs.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

Hard-counters: Luna, Jing, Diaochan, Li Bai — any hyper-mobile carry.
*Waiting for community input…*
donghuang.tips:
# Donghuang Taiyi Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Clockwise Walking = Double Healing

Orbs rotate **counter-clockwise**. Walk **clockwise** around enemies to make orbs contact them faster — doubles your healing and damage output. This is why Donghuang wins early jungle 1v2s.

### 2. S3: HP Trade — You Must Be Tankier

S3 links HP between Donghuang and the target. If you have MORE HP, you win the trade. If the enemy has MORE HP, you die. **Never suppress a tank with more HP than you.** Always target squishy carries.

### 3. Tower Kill Exploit

When an enemy is under their tower: **S2 → Flash → S3** on them. Every tower shot you take = free damage to the enemy. The tower kills them for you.

### 4. S2 Before S3 = Free Damage

S2 throws all orbs. S3 **instantly refills 3 orbs**. Always sequence: **S2 → S3**, not S3 → S2. You get the S2 CC/damage PLUS full orbs during suppression.

### 5. Ping Before Engaging

Donghuang has zero burst. If you Flash+S3 but your team doesn't follow up, you're trading 1-for-1 at best. **Spam "Attack" pings before going in.** In solo queue, make sure your carries are close enough to capitalize.

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Pickoff: **S2 → Flash → S3**

1. Charge 3 orbs
2. S2 throw orbs toward enemy (slow/stun)
3. **Flash → S3** directly onto the carry
4. Team follows up and deletes them during the 2.2s suppression

### Bush Ambush: **S3 → S2 → S1 → AA**

1. Wait in bush with 3 orbs
2. S3 as enemy walks past
3. S2 after suppression ends (slow + stun)
4. S1 → AA while walking clockwise for max orb hits

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S3 cannot be cleansed nor blocked by Unstoppable.** It overrides EVERYTHING except death.
- **Pick into hyper-mobile Heroes.** Donghuang is the ultimate Luna, Jing, Diaochan, Li Bai counter.
- **Don't pick into poke comps.** Donghuang struggles against long-range teams that never let him get close.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
donghuang.build:
# Donghuang Taiyi Build & Items

> **Build philosophy:** Stack **maximum HP** — more HP = you win S3 HP trades. Needs **Movement Speed** (to reach targets) and adequate **defenses**. Pure HP tank.

---

## Recommended Full-Tank Build

| 1 | **Shadow Aegis · Redemption (极影·救赎)** or **Guard · Redemption (近卫·救赎)** | Start Knowledge Gem. Upgrade mid-game. **Redemption active:** group shield — pop it during S3 when you're about to die; the shield absorbs mirrored damage. |

| 3 | **Overlord's Platemail (霸者重装)** | **Donghuang's #1 core item!** Highest base HP in the game. Win every S3 HP trade. In-combat + out-of-combat regen = never recall. |
| 4 | **Ominous Premonition (不祥征兆)** | Physical Defense + Max HP. Reduces enemy AS/MS on-hit — survival after S3 ends. |
| 5 | **Eternal Night (永夜守护)** or **Magic Cloak (魔女斗篷)** | **Eternal Night:** burst-heal on magic damage — perfect S3 synergy. **Magic Cloak:** sustained magic defense + spell shield. |
| 6 | **Blazing Cape (红莲斗篷)** or **Sage's Sanctuary (贤者的庇护)** | Blazing Cape: burn aura for melee damage. Sanctuary: resurrection for final fights. |

---

## Arcana / Runes (铭文)

Core needs: **Max HP (S3 win condition), CDR, Movement Speed.** Zero offensive stats needed.

### Setup 1: "Thousand-HP Tank" (CN Standard)

| 🟢 Green | Void (虚空) | ×10 | Max Health +37.5, Cooldown Reduction +0.6% |
| 🔵 Blue | Harmony (调和) | ×10 | Max Health +45, Move Speed +0.4%, HP/5s +5.2 |

**Total:** **+1,162 Max Health**, +23 Physical Defense, +4% MS, +6% CDR, +10% AS. Level 1 invade dominance.

### Setup 2: "Max Speed Chase"

| Slot | Rune | Qty | Stats per Rune |
|------|------|-----|----------------|

dr-bian.overview:
# Dr Bian（扁鵲） — Mage

## Overview

- **Role:** Mage (Sustained Poison / Healer)
- **Lane:** Mid Lane
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 善惡怪醫 (Good & Evil Doctor)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 扁鹊)

> Dr Bian is a sustained-damage "Mage-Tank" who balances poison DPS with team healing. His passive stacks marks (max 5): black marks on enemies deal continuous poison damage, white marks on allies provide continuous healing. He's a walking "poison + clinic" — the longer the fight, the stronger he becomes.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Mark Stacks: 5 Is Everything

Damage and healing scale with mark count. **5 stacks is dramatically stronger than 4 stacks.** In teamfights: stack marks to 5 on priority targets BEFORE casting S3.

### S3: Detonate + Refresh

S3 detonates all marks nearby, dealing burst damage/healing based on stack count. **S3 also refreshes the duration of all marks.** Use S3 as: execute on 5-stacked enemies, massive team heal, or mark refresher mid-fight.

### S2: Spam Constantly

S2 can be cast without a target. **Spam S2 while rotating** — this keeps 5 white marks on yourself and nearby allies at all times. You enter every fight pre-buffed with healing already ticking.

### S1: Zone Control + Stack

S1's bottles can be stored (max 3). Don't dump all 3 in one spot — spread them in a line to block retreat paths, zone enemies, or fast-clear waves.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dr-bian.tips:
# Dr Bian Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Stack to 5 Before S3

S3's burst scales with mark count. 5 stacks = massive damage/healing. 4 stacks = underwhelming. **Never S3 at <5 stacks unless it's an emergency.** Weave AAs between abilities to reach 5 faster.

### 2. Spam S2 While Rotating

S2 works without a target. Walking between lanes or through jungle? **Spam S2 constantly.** This keeps 5 healing marks on yourself AND nearby allies. You enter every fight with healing already ticking.

### 3. S1: Zone, Don't Dump

S1 bottles stack up to 3. Don't throw all 3 in one spot. Place them **in a line** across enemy retreat paths or choke points. Enemies must walk through poison or go around — either way, you win.

### 4. S3 Double Duty

S3 detonates marks AND refreshes their duration. Two uses:
- **Burst:** 5-stacked enemies → S3 → execute
- **Sustain:** 5-stacked allies → S3 → massive team heal + mark refresh

### 5. AA Between Everything

Dr Bian's AAs apply marks. The difference between 4 and 5 stacks is enormous. **Always weave AAs:** S1 → AA → S2 → AA. This guarantees 4+ stacks on contact.

---

## Signature Combos

### Lane Trade: **S1 → AA → S2 → AA**

1. S1 bottle (slow + 1 mark)
2. AA (2 marks)
3. S2 (3 marks)
4. AA (4-5 marks)
5. Most laners can't out-trade a 5-stack Dr Bian

### Teamfight: **S1 + S2 (AoE) → AA weave → S3 (detonate)**

1. S1 bottles + S2 into enemy cluster
2. AA to push key targets to 5 marks
3. S3 detonate — AoE burst + ally heal + mark refresh

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Attack Speed is mandatory.** AA is your fastest mark stacker. Without AS, you're too slow.
- **Semi-tank build only.** Pure AP Dr Bian dies before stacking to 5. Prophet's Hour + tank items keep you alive.
- **Matchup:** God-tier in extended fights. Struggles vs burst that kills before you stack.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

dun.overview:
# Dun（夏侯惇） — Tank / Fighter

## Overview

- **Role:** Tank / Fighter (All-Rounder Bruiser)
- **Lane:** Clash Lane / Jungle
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 不羈之風 (Unruly Wind)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 夏侯惇)

> Dun is an extremely versatile, ultra-tanky Clash Lane Fighter/Jungler. He has everything: CC, mobility, healing, shields, and True Damage. He's often called the "perfect Hero" in the CN server for his complete kit. His signature mechanic is his **passive lifesteal** — when HP drops below 50%, his AAs and abilities restore massive HP, letting him reverse-kill from seemingly certain death.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Passive: Vampiric Regeneration (Reverse-Kill Engine)

When HP drops **below 50%**, Dun enters a state where **every AA and ability hit restores 4.5% of his Max HP** for 8 seconds. Never retreat when this triggers — fight minions, monsters, or enemies and watch your HP refill. This is the "medical miracle" mechanic.

### S2: True Damage + Shield + CDR

S2 grants a **12% Max HP shield** and empowers the next 3 AAs with **True Damage**. Each empowered AA also **reduces S3 cooldown by 1 second**. S2 is your bread and butter — max it first.

### S3: Pseudo-Unstoppable Engage

S3 throws a chain blade and dashes to the target. **During the dash, Dun is CC-immune.** Stuns enemies at the impact point. Also crosses walls — use for engage AND escape.

### S1: Two-Stage Knockup

S1's first hit must connect (Hero, Minion, or Monster) to unlock the second stage — a wide AoE knockup. Poke minions to unlock stage 2, then threaten the enemy laner with the knockup.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dun.tips:
# Dun Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Passive: Never Retreat Below 50% HP

When HP drops below 50%, Dun enters lifesteal state for 8s: **every AA/ability hit restores 4.5% Max HP.** Don't run — fight! AA minions, monsters, or enemies. One wave can take you from 10% to 80% HP. This is the "medical miracle" that turns losing fights into reverse-kills.

### 2. S2 True Damage + S3 CDR

S2 grants a shield + 3 empowered AAs (True Damage). **Each empowered AA reduces S3 cooldown by 1 second.** Always S2 → AA×3 in fights — you'll have S3 back up almost immediately.

### 3. S3: Engage AND Escape

S3's dash is **CC-immune** during travel. Use to:
- **Engage:** S3 onto enemy backline (ignore their CC)
- **Escape:** S3 over walls when collapsed on
- **Outplay:** S3 through Daji's charm, Mozi's cannon

### 4. S1 Stage 2 Unlock

S1 stage 2 (AoE knockup) only unlocks if stage 1 hits something. Hit minions to unlock stage 2, then zone the enemy with the threat of the knockup.

### 5. Skill Priority

**Max S2 first, then S1. Take S3 whenever available.** S2's shield and True Damage are your laning and jungling foundation.

---

## Signature Combos

### Long-Range Engage: **S3 → S1 → AA → S2 → AA×3 → S1 (stage 2)**

1. S3 onto enemy (stun)
2. S1 stage 1 (slow)
3. AA
4. S2 (shield + True Damage)
5. AA×3 (True Damage + reduce S3 CD)
6. S1 stage 2 (AoE knockup)

### Bush Ambush: **S1 → AA → S2 → AA×3 → S1 (stage 2) → S3**

1. S1 from bush (slow)
2. AA → S2 shield + True Damage AAs
3. S1 stage 2 knockup
4. S3 to chase or finish

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Passive + Eternal Night = absurd healing.** The two effects stack, making Dun nearly unkillable at low HP.
- **S3 crosses walls.** Use it to escape ganks or steal objectives.
- **Tank Jungle Dun is meta in pro play.** Giant's Grip + Blazing Cape dual-burn clears camps absurdly fast.
- **Matchup:** Excels in extended brawls. Struggles vs burst that kills before passive triggers.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
dun.build:

---

## Build A: Clash Lane Flash Bruiser

| 1 | **Resistance Boots (抵抗之靴)** | Tenacity + magic defense. Keeps Dun mobile during engages. |

| 5 | **Eternal Night (永夜守护)** | Burst-heal on magic damage. Stacks with passive lifesteal — absurd low-HP survival. |

dun-shan.overview:
# Dun Shan（盾山） — Tank / Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Tank / Support (Anti-Projectile / Engage)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 無盡之盾 (Endless Shield)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 盾山)

> Dun Shan is a top-tier utility Tank who counters most Marksmen and Mages in the game. His core strength is **blocking all projectile-based attacks** (auto-attacks, spell projectiles), combined with devastating Flash-engage crowd control. He can also **repair allied turrets** while invulnerable.

---

## Core Mechanics

### Shield: Blocks Projectiles (S1 / S3)

Dun Shan's shield (S1 hold or S3 ultimate) completely blocks all projectile attacks — basic attacks, Zhuge Liang's ultimate, Diaochan's orbs, Hou Yi's arrows. **Each blocked projectile costs 1 Energy.** Energy runs out → shield drops.

### What the Shield Does NOT Block

Non-projectile ground/overhead abilities pierce the shield: Zhou Yu's fire, Wang Zhaojun's freeze, Zhong Kui's hook, Lu Bu's true damage. Know when to lower the shield.

### Repair Turret (S4)

Dun Shan can merge into an allied turret, becoming **invulnerable** while healing the turret and boosting its defenses. Use when: defending high ground while behind, or escaping death at low HP.

### S2: Shoulder Throw (Core Engage)

S2 charges forward and **throws an enemy over his shoulder** behind him. Combined with Flash, this is one of the most dangerous engage tools in the game.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

**Pick Dun Shan into:** Baili Shouyue, Zhuge Liang, Hou Yi, Daji, Shangguan. **Avoid vs:** Zhou Yu, Wang Zhaojun, Lu Bu, Zhang Liang.
*Waiting for community input…*
dun-shan.tips:
# Dun Shan Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. Shield: What It Blocks (and What It Doesn't)

**Blocks (S1/S3 immune):** All projectile auto-attacks, Zhuge Liang S3, Diaochan orbs, Hou Yi arrows, Angela S2, Daji charm.
**Does NOT block (shield is useless):** Zhou Yu fire, Wang Zhaojun freeze, Zhong Kui hook, Lu Bu true damage, ground-targeted AoE.

### 2. S2 + Flash: The Signature Engage

S2 has a brief charge forward. **Flash during the charge** to extend range massively. This "Flash Shoulder Throw" can kidnap enemy carries from a screen away and dump them into your team.

### 3. S3 Ultimate: Zone Control

S3 creates a giant shield wall that pushes enemies back. Can't move during. Use in:
- **Narrow jungle corridors:** Split enemy frontline from backline
- **Dragon/Baron pit:** Trap enemies against the wall
- **After S2 Flash:** Immediately S3 after throwing to trap the victim

### 4. S4 Repair Turret

Merge into allied turrets: you're invulnerable, the turret heals + gains defense. Use to:
- Defend high ground while behind
- Escape death when low HP
- Stall for teammates to respawn

### 5. Skill Priority

**Max S2 first, then S1. Take ultimate whenever available.** S2 lower cooldown = more shoulder throws = more kills.

---

## Signature Combo

### Flash Kidnap Combo: **S2 → Flash → S3 → S1**

1. S2 charge toward enemy
2. **Flash mid-charge** to extend range
3. S2 completes — enemy thrown behind you into your team
4. **Immediately S3** to push them further and wall them in
5. S1 shield to block any rescue attempts

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Energy management:** Each blocked projectile costs 1 Energy. Against rapid-fire Marksmen (Hou Yi, Marco), your shield drains fast. Lower it preemptively to recharge.
- **Counter-pick hero:** Dun Shan is a hard counter to projectile-reliant teams. If the enemy has 3+ projectile Heroes, lock him in.
- **Avoid vs:** Zhou Yu, Wang Zhaojun, Lu Bu — their AoE ground damage ignores your shield entirely.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

dyadia.overview:
# Dyadia（少司緣） — Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Support (Economy / Utility Enchanter)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★☆☆☆
- **Title:** 聆願之祝 (Listener's Blessing)
- **Region:** CN server exclusive (少司缘)

> Dyadia is a uniquely tactical utility Support. Her core mechanic: **two-phase abilities (apply mark → reactivate to fly to target).** S1 marks allies (heals, then fly to them for massive heal). S2 marks enemies (**steals Gold per second**, then fly to them for a root + double Gold payout). She's the only Hero in the game who can directly manipulate the economic gap between teams.

---

## Core Mechanics

### S1: Good Bond (Ally)

- **Phase 1:** Apply "Good Bond" to an ally — continuous healing over time.
- **Phase 2 (reactivate):** Fly to that ally, burst-heal them, and grant +MS to both.

### S2: Evil Bond (Enemy — The Gold Stealer)

- **Phase 1:** Apply "Evil Bond" to an enemy — continuous Magic Damage + **steals 1 Gold per second** (max 5). Free economic advantage!
- **Phase 2 (reactivate):** Fly to that enemy, **root them**, and **doubles the stolen Gold.** If the enemy dies while marked, ALL stolen Gold drops.

### S3: Karmic Melody (Massive AoE)

S3 sends a wave that: burst-heals allies with Good Bond marks, and **extends root duration + instantly collects all stolen Gold** from enemies with Evil Bond marks.

### Enhanced AA After Phase 2

Successfully completing a Phase 2 (flying to ally or enemy) grants an **enhanced AA with extended range + self-dash.** Use to: escape after a risky S2 dive, or gap-close after S1 flight.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

*Waiting for community input…*
dyadia.tips:
# Dyadia Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S2 Phase 1 Only: Safe Free Gold

You don't HAVE to Phase 2 every time. Apply S2 to the enemy ADC/Support → walk away. Free 5 Gold stolen with zero risk. Repeat on cooldown. Over 5 minutes, you've created a significant economic gap.

### 2. S2 → S1 → Enhanced AA: The Hit-and-Run

1. S2 Phase 1 on enemy
2. S2 Phase 2 → fly to them (root)
3. Enhanced AA (dash backward)
4. S1 on your ADC → Phase 2 → fly back to safety
5. You've rooted the enemy, dealt damage, stolen Gold, and returned to your ADC safely

### 3. S3: Combine Both Marks

Optimal S3: have S1 on an ally AND S2 on an enemy. S3 heals your ally (burst) AND extends the enemy's root + collects Gold. Double value.

### 4. S2 Phase 2: Only When Safe

Flying to an enemy puts you in melee range. Only Phase 2 when: the enemy is isolated, your team is with you, or you have S1 on an ally to fly back immediately after.

### 5. S1 Phase 2: Emergency Save

When your ADC is about to die: S1 Phase 2 → fly to them → massive burst heal + MS boost. This can save them from execute ults (Zhuge Liang S3, Kongming S3).

---

## Signature Combos

### Gold Steal Harass: **S2 (Phase 1 only) → repeat**

1. S2 enemy ADC. Wait 5 seconds. 5 Gold stolen.
2. Repeat on cooldown.
3. By 5 minutes, your ADC is 300+ Gold ahead.

### Full Teamfight Cycle: **S2 → S1 → S3 → S2 Phase 2 → Enhanced AA → S1 Phase 2**

1. S2 Phase 1 on enemy frontline
2. S1 Phase 1 on your ADC
3. S3 (burst-heals ADC + extends S2 mark)
4. S2 Phase 2 (root enemy + double Gold)
5. Enhanced AA dash back
6. S1 Phase 2 (fly to ADC safely)

---

## Advanced Tips

- **Gold stolen is REAL.** It directly reduces the enemy's gold. This is the only economic warfare mechanic in the game.
- **S2 can be cast on minions for Phase 2 mobility.** Use S2 on a minion → Phase 2 → free dash.
- **Matchup:** God-tier in extended laning phases. Struggles vs heavy dive that kills before Phase 2 completes.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!

eclipse.overview:
# Eclipse（鬼谷子） — Support

## Overview

- **Role:** Support (Engage / Tempo-Setter)
- **Lane:** Roaming
- **Difficulty:** ★★★☆☆
- **Title:** 萬物有靈 (Spirit of All Things)
- **Region:** Global (CN server name: 鬼谷子)

> Eclipse is an extremely aggressive, tempo-setting Support with one of the game's few team-wide Camouflage mechanics. He excels at leading his team in stealth for surprise "blitzkrieg" engages. His signature: group stealth → group pull → group stun → team-wipe.

---

## Core Mechanics

### S1: Camouflage + Defense Shred

Enter Camouflage with +40% MS. **Enemies you touch during stealth lose 25% Physical & Magic Defense.** Don't just use S1 to travel — use it to tag enemies before your team engages.

### S2: Group Pull + Stun

After 2 seconds, pulls all enemies in range toward Eclipse + **1s stun**. If an enemy Hero is pulled, all nearby allies gain a stackable shield. This is your game-winning ability.

### S3: Team-Wide Stealth + Vision

Grant Camouflage + 30% MS to ALL nearby allies. Also **reveals the nearest enemy Hero** — hard-counters Lanling Wang, Ake, and bush ambushes.

### Golden Rule: Don't AA During S1

Auto-attacking or casting other abilities **cancels S1's Camouflage**. Correct sequence: S1 → touch enemies to shred defense → THEN S2 to pull.

---

## Recommended Build

See [`build.md`](build.md) for full item builds and Arcana loadouts.

## Counters & Synergies

Best paired with: AoE burst Mages/Marksmen who follow up on pulls.
*Waiting for community input…*
eclipse.tips:
# Eclipse Tips & Tricks

---

## Core Gameplay Tips

### 1. S1: Don't Auto-Attack During Stealth

S1 Camouflage cancels if you AA or cast other abilities. Correct flow:
1. **S1 → touch enemies (defense shred applied)**
2. **Then S2 → pull**
Never AA during S1 — it breaks the stealth.

### 2. S2 Fake-Out Charge

S2 has a 2-second delay before pulling. Without Flash, don't run straight at enemies — they'll just walk away. Instead:
1. Start S2 from a bush
2. Let it charge for ~1.5 seconds
3. S1 or Wolf Rush (support item) to dash into enemies at the last instant
4. S2 pops right as you arrive — instant pull

### 3. S3: Vision + Stealth = Anti-Ambush

S3 reveals the nearest enemy Hero. Use proactively:
- Before face-checking Dragon/Baron pit
- When Lanling Wang or Ake might be nearby
- To spot bush campers before your team walks in

### 4. S2 + Flash: Undodgeable Engage

S2 → charge for 1.5s → **Flash directly onto the enemy backline** at the last instant. S2 pops, stunning everyone. The Flash makes this nearly impossible to react to.

### 5. Communication Is Everything

Eclipse is useless if teammates don't follow up. When you're about to engage: **ping "Attack" or "On my way."** If your Mid/ADC are scaling champions (Garo, Daji), they may not have enough damage early — adjust your aggression accordingly.

---

## Signature Combo

### Lightning War Engage: **S3 → Wolf Rush → S1 → S2 → Flash**

1. **S3:** Team-wide stealth + MS
2. **Wolf Rush (support item):** Extra team speed burst
3. **S1:** +40% MS, tag enemies for defense shred
4. **S2:** Charge for 1.5s
5. **Flash:** Dive into enemy backline at the last instant
6. **S2 pops:** Multiple enemies pulled + stunned → team cleanup

---

## Advanced Tips

- **S2 shield stacks per Hero pulled.** A 3-man pull = massive team shield.
- **Counter-pick into immobile comps.** Eclipse destroys teams without dashes. Struggles vs highly mobile teams that dodge S2.
- **Don't over-engage without S3.** Eclipse's engage without team stealth is predictable and easily dodged.

---

> ⚠ **Player-discovered tips.** Contribute your own at the [community repo](#)!
eclipse.build:
# Eclipse Build & Items

> **Build philosophy:** Eclipse is squishy and roams constantly. Needs max **CDR**, max **Movement Speed**, and enough **tankiness** to survive rushing into 5 enemies.

---

## Recommended Tempo-Setting Build

| Order | Item | Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| 1 | **Shadow Aegis · Wolf Rush (极影·奔狼)** | **Core support item.** Wolf Rush active: team-wide speed burst. Stacks with S3 + S1 for lightning-fast engages. |
| 2 | **Swift Boots (疾步之靴)** | Must be the fastest Hero on the map. Swift Boots + S1 = unmatched roaming. |
| 3 | **Glacial Storm (极寒风暴)** | **20% CDR** + Physical Defense. Frost shockwave on burst helps Eclipse escape after pulling. |
| 4 | **Magic Cloak (魔女斗篷)** | Magic Defense + spell shield. Prevents getting poked out while charging engages. |
| 5 | **Ominous Premonition (不祥征兆)** | Physical Defense + Max HP. Reduces enemy AS/MS on-hit — helps survival after diving in. |
| 6 | **Eternal Night (永夜守护)** or **Overlord's Platemail (霸者重装)** | **Eternal Night:** vs magic burst. **Overlord's Platemail:** infinite sustain — never recall, constant map pressure. |

---

## Arcana / Runes (铭文)

Core needs: **Movement Speed (catch enemies), CDR (skill uptime), base tankiness.**

### Setup 1: "Max Speed Hunt" (CN Standard)

| Slot | Rune | Qty | Stats per Rune |

| 🔵 Blue | Hunt (狩猎) | ×10 | Attack Speed +1%, Move Speed +1% |

**Total:** +712 Max Health, +23 Physical Defense, +6% CDR, **+10% Movement Speed**, +20% Attack Speed. Max roaming tempo from level 1.

### Setup 2: "10% CDR Pull Spam"

| Slot | Rune | Qty | Stats per Rune |
|------|------|-----|----------------|
| 🔴 Red | Destiny (宿命) | ×10 | Max Health +33.7, Physical Defense +2.3, Attack Speed +1% |
| 🟢 Green | Compassion (怜悯) | ×10 | Cooldown Reduction +1% |
| 🔵 Blue | Harmony (调和) + Hunt (狩猎) | ×5 each | Health + MS mix |

**Total:** +337 Max Health, +23 Physical Defense, **+10% CDR**, +4~7% MS. S2 available every few seconds — more pull attempts, more opportunities.

---

## Battlefield Mindset

Eclipse is **team-dependent**. If your carries are scaling champions (Garo, Daji), they may lack early damage to follow your engages. Ping "Attack" before going in. If your team doesn't respond, don't force it — wait for the right window.
